title: Referral and Subscription Fees for Amazon Sellers
summary: Amazon charges a referral fee on each sale that varies by product category, plus a selling-plan subscription fee. Most categories carry a per-item minimum referral fee.
content:
- Two selling plans: an Individual plan charged per item sold and a Professional plan charged as a flat monthly subscription.
- A referral fee is deducted on every item sold, calculated as a percentage of the total sale price or a minimum amount, whichever is greater.
- Referral percentages differ by category; some categories use tiered rates based on price thresholds.
- A per-item minimum referral fee applies to most categories, with limited exceptions (e.g., gift cards, some media).
- Optional services such as FBA and advertising add separate costs.

title: Account Health Performance Thresholds
summary: Sellers must keep core performance metrics within Amazon's targets to avoid restrictions or deactivation. Key metrics include Order Defect Rate, Late Shipment Rate, and Cancellation Rate.
content:
- Order Defect Rate (ODR) must stay under 1% to avoid restrictions on selling privileges.
- Late Shipment Rate (LSR) for seller-fulfilled orders should remain below 4%.
- Pre-fulfillment Cancellation Rate should stay below 2.5%.
- The Account Health dashboard (under the Performance tab) reflects deactivation risk; an 'At Risk' or 'Critical' rating signals possible account deactivation.

title: Restricted and Prohibited Product Compliance
summary: Sellers must check Amazon's restricted product lists before listing, obtain pre-approval where required, and never list counterfeit or infringing goods.
content:
- Review restricted-products lists before listing any item.
- Certain categories require pre-approval before a seller may list in them.
- Counterfeit products are forbidden.
- Listing items that infringe others' intellectual property is prohibited.
- Violations such as listing counterfeit goods or providing false information can lead to listing removal or account deactivation.

title: Seller-Fulfilled Returns and Refund Handling
summary: Sellers cannot impose a blanket no-returns policy; Amazon's return rules supersede seller policies. Returns requiring authorization must be handled within set timelines and refunds issued promptly.
content:
- A 'no returns' or blanket-refusal policy is not permitted; Amazon's 30-day return window applies to most items regardless of who fulfills the order.
- Return requests needing seller authorization (e.g., outside standard policy, hazardous/restricted items) must be responded to within about 24 hours.
- Refunds must be issued within roughly two days of receiving the returned item, or Amazon refunds on the seller's behalf.
- Sellers may configure return-less refunds (customer keeps the item) for suitable low-cost cases.
- Ignoring return requests or refusing return labels can trigger automatic customer refunds and possible account suspension.

title: Main Product Image Requirements
summary: Main product images must meet Amazon's technical specs: a pure white background, sufficient resolution, and the product filling most of the frame.
content:
- The longest side should be between 500 and 10,000 pixels; over 1,000 pixels per side is preferred to enable zoom.
- The main image requires a pure white background (RGB 255,255,255).
- The product should fill roughly 85% or more of the frame.
- At least one image is required; several images from different angles are recommended.
- Accepted formats include JPEG, TIFF, PNG, and non-animated GIF; images must be clear and free of jagged or pixelated edges.

title: Brand Registry Trademark and Enrollment Requirements
summary: To enroll in Amazon Brand Registry, a brand needs an active or pending registered trademark, and the application must be submitted by the trademark owner with brand details matching the trademark record.
content:
- An active registered trademark, or a pending registration, from an approved government IP office is required (text/word mark or design mark with words, letters, or numbers).
- The brand name must match the trademark record exactly (capitalization may differ, but spaces and symbols must match).
- The brand name must be permanently affixed to the product (e.g., printed, sewn, etched, engraved); stickers, labels, stamps, and tags are not accepted.
- Stock images, digitally altered images, and mockups are not accepted.
- Applications must be submitted by the legal trademark owner; Amazon verifies ownership via a code sent to the trademark's public contact.

title: Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Operational Requirements
summary: FBA-enrolled sellers must verify product eligibility, prepare and label inventory per Amazon's workflow, and comply with restock and storage limits to avoid extra charges.
content:
- Most products qualify for FBA, but some are ineligible or need prior approval; review FBA product restrictions before enrolling.
- Inventory must be prepared, packed, and labeled using the 'Send to Amazon' workflow before shipping to fulfillment centers.
- Sellers must comply with restock limits governing maximum inventory volume.
- Aged-inventory charges apply to stock held beyond 181 days; storage fees are based on daily average cubic footage.
- Additional fees may apply for returns processing, removal, disposal, and liquidation.
